issues.title: AArch64 PAC return-address signing removed from default compilation flags
issues.problem: The `-DHITLS_AARCH64_PACIASP` flag was removed from CC_DEFINE_FLAGS. This disables Pointer Authentication (PAC) for all ARMv8 assembly code (AES, SHA, SM2/3/4, ECC, ML-KEM, etc.). PAC is a significant security hardening feature that helps prevent ROP (Return-Oriented Programming) attacks on ARMv.3-A+ processors. This change affects not just the new ML-KEM code but ALL existing ARMv8 assembly implementations.

issues.title: x2 SHAKE path lacks big-endian byte order conversion
issues.problem: The new NEON x2 SHAKE absorb/squeeze path loads and stores 64-bit lanes using `vld1_u8`/`vst1_u8` without little-endian conversion. The existing SHA3 ARMv8 assembly in `crypto/sha3/src/asm/sha3_armv8.S` handles `HITLS_BIG_ENDIAN`, and the noasm version in `noasm_sha3.c` uses `GET_UINT64_LE`/`PUT_UINT64_LE` macros. This new C path will produce incorrect SHAKE output on big-endian targets.

issues.title: Direct SHA3/SHAKE calls bypass provider dispatch in keygen/encrypt
issues.problem: Key generation and encryption now call direct one-shot functions `CRYPT_SHA3_512()` and `CRYPT_SHAKE256()`, bypassing `ctx->libCtx` provider dispatch. This breaks consistency with the provider-based architecture used elsewhere in the codebase and violates provider/FIPS routing expectations. Error paths that were previously handled via EAL MD are now silently ignored.

issues.title: SampleEta1/2 use direct SHAKE calls, bypassing provider dispatch
issues.problem: `SampleEta1` and `SampleEta2` now use direct `CRYPT_SHAKE256` one-shot calls instead of provider-aware EAL MD calls. This bypasses `ctx->libCtx` provider dispatch, removes error propagation capability, and is inconsistent with the provider-based architecture.

issues.title: Buffer cleanse length mismatch for kr buffer
issues.problem: The `kr` buffer is declared as `uint8_t kr[CRYPT_SHA3_512_DIGESTSIZE + 1]` (65 bytes) to accommodate the extra nonce byte written by `SampleEta2`. However, `BSL_SAL_CleanseData(kr, CRYPT_SHA3_512_DIGESTSIZE)` only wipes 64 bytes, leaving one byte (the nonce) uncleansed on the stack.
issues.code:
uint8_t kr[CRYPT_SHA3_512_DIGESTSIZE + 1];    // K and r (+1 for nonce)
...
ret = HashFuncG(ctx->libCtx, mhek, MLKEM_SEED_LEN + CRYPT_SHA3_256_DIGESTSIZE, kr, CRYPT_SHA3_512_DIGESTSIZE);
...
BSL_SAL_CleanseData(kr, CRYPT_SHA3_512_DIGESTSIZE);  // Only wipes 64 bytes
issues.fix:
uint8_t kr[CRYPT_SHA3_512_DIGESTSIZE + 1];    // K and r (+1 for nonce)
...
ret = HashFuncG(ctx->libCtx, mhek, MLKEM_SEED_LEN + CRYPT_SHA3_256_DIGESTSIZE, kr, CRYPT_SHA3_512_DIGESTSIZE);
...
BSL_SAL_CleanseData(kr, sizeof(kr));  // Wipes all 65 bytes

issues.title: Variable Length Array may cause stack overflow in deep call stacks
issues.problem: The `s_asym` variable is declared as a VLA with size `k * (MLKEM_N >> 1)` elements. For k=4, this is 1024 bytes on stack. Combined with `e[MLKEM_K_MAX][MLKEM_N]` (2048 bytes) and other local variables including Keccakx2State, total stack usage could exceed 2KB. This may cause issues in embedded contexts or deep call stacks with limited stack space.

issues.title: Potentially unaligned NEON load may impact performance
issues.problem: The `vld1q_u64(tmp0)` loads from a stack-allocated array `tmp0[2]` which may not be 16-byte aligned on all ARMv8 implementations. While ARMv8 supports unaligned loads, they may be slower than aligned loads. This occurs in the hot path of the absorb function and could impact performance.
